title: Verb сѓѕсЂєсЂе VerbсѓІ сЂЙсЂёсЂе
titleEn: This structure indicates that regardless of whether one does (verb) or not, the outcome/attitude remains unchanged.
explanation: This structure indicates that regardless of whether one does (verb) or not, the outcome/attitude remains unchanged. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The expression 'Verb-volitional form + сЂе + Verb-negative volitional (№йъсЂЙсЂё) + сЂе' means 'regardless of whether one does X or not, it doesn't change the situation'. It shows the speaker's firm stance or the unchanging nature of some outcome.

title: Verb сѓѕсЂєсЂФсѓѓ
titleEn: Used to express 'even though one wants to do something, one can't for some reason'.
explanation: Used to express 'even though one wants to do something, one can't for some reason'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The №йъсѓѕсЂєсЂФсѓѓ grammar point indicates that a person wants or tries to do something (volitional form), but for some reason cannot. Often, there's a second clause explaining what prevents the action.

title: Verb сѓѕсЂєсЂФсѓѓ Verb сѓїсЂфсЂё
titleEn: Expresses the inability to do something even if one wants/tries.
structure: Verb-volitional form + сЂФсѓѓ + (potential negative form / reason)
structureEn: Verb-volitional form + сЂФсѓѓ + (potential negative form / reason)
explanation: Expresses the inability to do something even if one wants/tries. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: This grammar shows that you want (or try) to do something (volitional form), but cannot due to some obstacle. Often it appears as 'Verb-volitional + сЂФсѓѓ + Verb (potential negative)' or simply 'Verb-volitional + сЂФсѓѓ + (reason you can't)'.

title: Verb сѓѕсЂєсѓѓсЂфсЂё
explanation: Expresses 'it's impossible to do something'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: №йъсѓѕсЂєсѓѓсЂфсЂё typically attaches to the verb's сЂЙсЂЎ-stem, adding сѓѕсЂєсѓѓсЂфсЂё to indicate that there is 'no way' or 'no method' to perform the action. ItРђЎs the same nuance as 'VerbсѓѕсЂєсЂїсЂфсЂё' in many references.

title: VerbсѓІ сЂїсЂЙсЂЙсЂФ
titleEn: Expresses 'letting something happen as it is'; 'just as something does'.
explanation: Expresses 'letting something happen as it is'; 'just as something does'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: сЂїсЂЙсЂЙсЂФ describes an action happening exactly 'as is,' without intervention or change, often suggesting doing something passively or naturally.

title: VerbсѓІ сЂїТЌЕсЂёсЂІ
titleEn: Expresses 'as soon as...' or 'the moment when...'.
explanation: Expresses 'as soon as...' or 'the moment when...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: VerbсѓІ сЂїТЌЕсЂёсЂІ indicates that something happens immediately after something else. It's similar to 'as soon as...' or 'no sooner than...'.

title: VerbсѓІ сЂЈсѓЅсЂёсЂфсѓЅ
titleEn: Expresses preference to avoid something: 'rather than...', 'if it comes to doing X, I'd rather...'.
explanation: Expresses preference to avoid something: 'rather than...', 'if it comes to doing X, I'd rather...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: This grammar is used when the speaker would rather not do something or chooses another option rather than face the stated action. ItРђЎs often translated as 'rather than... I'd prefer...' or 'if it comes down to doing X, I'd rather...'.

title: VerbсѓІ сЂЊсЂе сЂфсЂЌ сЂФ
explanation: Used to express 'without doing something'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: VerbсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂфсЂЌсЂФ indicates doing something else 'without' performing the stated action. It can imply skipping or omitting the verb action entirely.

title: VerbсѓІ сЂЊсЂесЂ«сЂфсЂёсѓѕсЂєсЂФ
titleEn: Expresses the desire or effort to prevent something: 'so as not to...', 'to avoid...'.
explanation: Expresses the desire or effort to prevent something: 'so as not to...', 'to avoid...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: VerbсѓІ сЂЊсЂесЂ«сЂфсЂёсѓѕсЂєсЂФ is used to show that one is trying or intending to prevent some action from happening. It's like 'so that this doesn't happen' or 'in order not to...'.

title: VerbсѓІ сЂесЂЇсѓісЂїсЂфсЂё
titleEn: Used to indicate that something can go on endlessly or without limits.
explanation: Used to indicate that something can go on endlessly or without limits. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The №йъсѓІсЂесЂЇсѓісЂїсЂфсЂё grammar point is used to express that once you start doing the verb, thereРђЎs no end or limit. The phrase 'сЂЇсѓісЂїсЂфсЂё' means 'no end' or 'endless.'
